title: Adding Automation Names to Item Activity Log
excerpt: Currently when you access an item or subitem’s activity log, you can see that an automation made changes to an item. When working with large scale automations and workflows, not knowing what automation or flow made the c…

posts.author: KristaBowman (Krista Bowman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Currently when you access an item or subitem’s activity log, you can see that an automation made changes to an item. When working with large scale automations and workflows, not knowing what automation or flow made the changes makes troubleshooting extremely hard.

Also, not being able to see the item values in the run history further exacerbates the issue.

posts.author: UFAI (Adam)
posts.cookedHtml:
I absolutely agree! So frustrating just to see a huge list of Automations and having to memorize all your workflows to figure out which automations ran out of order and screwed stuff up.
